response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer, but his profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a university thesis project, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His career centers on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and D. He has no SQL Server or Oracle experience (MySQL and MongoDB only), and no Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain background; his RIDE Capital stint was a Node/GraphQL fintech role, not capital markets systems work.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, desktop, and low-latency systems
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source contributions
Proven performance-oriented work (millions of requests/second, async I/O) and good communication via talks and workshops
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015), versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB
No Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge, and no Summit platform exposure

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain
Strong general engineering fundamentals and CI/CD practices
